_defaults.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu preferred; neat formal wear acceptable in outer areas. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta. Modest dress required.
_defaults.photography: Permitted in outer areas. Not allowed near sanctum during rituals.

temples.name: Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple
temples.description: One of the wealthiest temples in the world. The presiding deity Lord Padmanabha is seen in the Ananthshayana posture on the serpent Adi Shesha. Only Hindus are permitted to enter.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti (mundu) only — no shirts, no pants inside. Women: Saree or half-saree (set mundu). Western clothing strictly not allowed.
temples.photography: Strictly prohibited inside the temple premises. No cameras or mobile phones allowed.
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple, located at East Fort in Thiruvananthapuram,

temples.name: Guruvayur Sree Krishna Temple
temples.description: Known as the Dwarka of the South. One of the most important places of worship for Hindus in Kerala. The idol is made of Patala Anjana stone.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti only — no shirts inside sanctum. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta. No jeans or shorts.
temples.photography: Not permitted inside the temple. Photography allowed in outer courtyard.
temples.nearestRail: Guruvayur Railway Station (0.5 km) · Thrissur Railway Station (29 km)

temples.name: Sabarimala Sree Dharmasastha Temple
temples.timing: Seasonal: Nov–Jan (Mandalam & Makaravilakku) and Vishu season
temples.description: Most visited pilgrimage site in Kerala, situated on a hill at 914 m. Famous for the Makaravilakku festival. Pilgrims must observe 41-day Vratha before visiting.
temples.dressCode: All pilgrims must wear black or blue clothing (Irumudi). Casual clothing not permitted. Pilgrim's Irumudi (sacred bundle) is mandatory.
temples.photography: Photography permitted in outer areas. Strictly prohibited in sanctum sanctorum and during rituals.
temples.nearestBus: Nilackal / Pampa base camp — shuttle buses available from all Kerala districts
temples.nearestRail: Chengannur Railway Station (67 km) · Kottayam Railway Station (78 km)

temples.name: Attukal Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Known as the Sabarimala of Women. Holds the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of women for a religious event — Attukal Pongala, with over 3 million women participating.
temples.dressCode: Women: Saree or churidar. Men: Dhoti or trousers with shirt. Modest clothing expected.
temples.photography: Photography allowed in outer premises. Not permitted during rituals or inside sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Attukal Bus Stop (0.1 km) · Thiruvananthapuram City Bus Terminal (2 km)
temples.famousFor: Attukal temple is located at the heart of Thiruvananthapuram city in Attukal, has Goddess Bhadrakali (Kannaki) as the main deity.

temples.name: Vadakkunnathan Temple
temples.description: Ancient Shiva temple at the heart of Thrissur. Famous for Thrissur Pooram, the grandest temple festival in Kerala. UNESCO-recognized intangible cultural heritage.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti only. Women: Saree. Western clothing not allowed inside. Footwear removed at entrance.
temples.photography: Not permitted inside the temple. Permitted in the surrounding thekkinkadu maidan during Pooram.
temples.nearestBus: Thrissur KSRTC Bus Stand (0.8 km) · Round South Bus Stop (0.2 km)

temples.name: Ernakulathappan Temple
temples.description: The presiding deity of Ernakulam city. Famous for the grand Ernakulam Shiva Ratri Mahotsavam, celebrated over 8 days with lakhs of devotees.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred; clean trousers acceptable. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Ernakulam South Bus Stand (1 km) · High Court Junction Bus Stop (0.4 km)
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Ernakulathappan Temple at Ernakulam,

temples.name: Ettumanoor Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Famous Shiva temple known for the richly decorated golden flagmast and Ezharaponnana (seven and a half golden elephants). The temple murals are among the finest in Kerala.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu — no shirts inside. Women: Saree or half-saree. No jeans, shorts, or sleeveless tops.
temples.photography: Strictly prohibited inside. Murals and outer architecture may be photographed.

temples.name: Ambalapuzha Sree Krishna Temple
temples.description: Famous for Ambalapuzha Palpayasam — rice pudding offered to the deity daily. Legend says Lord Krishna played a chess game here and the offering continues as fulfillment of that promise.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti/mundu — shirts removed at sanctum. Women: Saree or half-saree. Modest attire required.
temples.photography: Not permitted inside the temple.

temples.name: Mannarasala Sree Nagaraja Temple
temples.description: Ancient serpent deity temple in a dense forest grove of 16 acres. Managed by a woman (Amma). Famous for the Ayilya festival and thousands of snake idols in the grove.
temples.dressCode: Men: Mundu/dhoti. Women: Saree or half-saree. No leather items (belts, wallets) inside premises.
temples.photography: Permitted in the forest grove. Restricted near sanctum and snake-idol areas.

temples.name: Aranmula Parthasarathy Temple
temples.description: Famous for the Aranmula Uthrittathi Vallamsankedam — a snake boat race held as a religious offering. One of the 108 Divya Desams of Lord Vishnu.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti mandatory — shirts not allowed in sanctum. Women: Saree or set mundu.
temples.photography: Not allowed inside. Boat race festival photography is permitted.
temples.nearestBus: Aranmula Bus Stand (0.5 km) · Chengannur Bus Stand (11 km)
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Aranmula Parthasarathy Temple in Pathanamthitta,

temples.name: Thirunelli Temple
temples.description: Ancient Vishnu temple deep in the Brahmagiri hills at 900 m elevation. Called 'Kashi of the South'. One of the holiest spots for performing ancestral rites (pitru tharpanam).
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti only inside sanctum. Women: Saree. Smart casual acceptable in outer areas.
temples.photography: Allowed in temple surroundings and forest. Not permitted inside sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Mananthavady Bus Stand (32 km) · Kalpetta Bus Stand (50 km)
temples.nearestRail: Kozhikode Railway Station (104 km) · Mysuru Railway Station (96 km)
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Thirunelli Mahavishnu Temple, one of the famous Lord Vishnu temples and a place famous where devotees perform ancestral rites, in Wayanad,

temples.name: Kottiyoor Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Remote forest temple on the banks of the Bavali River. Accessible only during the Vaisakha Mahotsavam season (28 days). No idol — Lord is worshipped as a naturally formed Shivalingam.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti only — no shirts. Women: Saree or half-saree. Pilgrim dress code strictly enforced.
temples.photography: Photography not permitted at the main shrine. Forest areas may be photographed.
temples.nearestBus: Kottiyoor Bus Stand (1 km) · Thalassery Bus Stand (52 km)
temples.nearestRail: Thalassery Railway Station (52 km) · Kannur Railway Station (60 km)

temples.name: Chettikulangara Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Powerful Bhagavathi temple. The Chettikulangara Bharani is among the grandest festivals in South Kerala, famous for the illuminated kuthira (horse) procession.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or trousers with shirt. Women: Saree or churidar. Modest dress mandatory.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer precincts. Not allowed near sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Chettikulangara Bus Stop (0.2 km) · Kollam KSRTC Bus Stand (8 km)
temples.famousFor: A page with video, photo and details of Chettikulangara Bhagavathy temple, situated in Mavelikkara taluk in Alappuzha district,

temples.name: Pazhavangadi Ganapathy Temple
temples.description: Famous Ganapathy temple adjacent to Padmanabhaswamy Temple. Known for the unique custom of offering coconuts. One of the most visited Ganesha temples in Kerala.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred; clean formal wear acceptable. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer areas. Not permitted inside sanctum.

temples.name: Thiruvarppu Krishna Temple
temples.description: Ancient Krishna temple on the banks of the Meenachil river. Mentioned in the Bhagavata Purana as one of Lord Krishna's sacred abodes.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu. Women: Saree or half-saree. Neat formal wear allowed in outer areas.
temples.photography: Not permitted inside the sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Thiruvarppu Bus Stop (0.5 km) · Kottayam KSRTC Bus Stand (9 km)

temples.name: Kaladi Sree Sharada Temple
temples.description: Located at the birthplace of Adi Shankaracharya. A powerful Saraswati and Sharada shrine attracting scholars, students, and devotees from across India.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat formals. Women: Saree or churidar. Shorts and sleeveless not allowed.
temples.photography: Allowed at the Adi Shankaracharya birthplace outside. Restricted inside temples.
temples.nearestBus: Kaladi Bus Stand (0.3 km) · Perumbavoor Bus Stand (10 km)

temples.name: Jatayu Earth's Center
temples.description: Unique hilltop temple complex featuring the world's largest bird sculpture — Jatayu, the mythical eagle from Ramayana. The 200-foot sculpture houses a museum inside.
temples.dressCode: No strict dress code; decent clothing expected. Comfortable footwear advised for hilly terrain.
temples.photography: Photography freely allowed throughout the complex. Drone photography requires special permission.
temples.nearestRail: Mukhathala Railway Station (7 km) · Kollam Railway Station (54 km)

temples.name: Thali Siva Temple
temples.description: 13th century temple built by the Zamorins of Kozhikode. A rare blend of Malabar and traditional Kerala temple architecture with intricate woodwork.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta. Modest dress strictly enforced.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Not permitted inside sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Kozhikode KSRTC Bus Stand (1 km) · Palayam Bus Stand (0.6 km)

temples.name: Mangaladevi Temple
temples.timing: Open only on Chithra Pournami (April–May full moon night)
temples.description: Remote forest temple in the Periyar Tiger Reserve at 1337 m elevation. Open to public only once a year on Chithra Pournami night. Surrounded by dense Cardamom Hills forests.
temples.dressCode: Traditional dress required on Chithra Pournami. Warm clothing advised due to altitude and night temperatures.
temples.photography: Restricted — forest reserve rules apply. Wildlife photography requires Forest Department permit.

temples.name: Thiruvambady Sri Krishna Temple
temples.description: One of the two primary temples of the spectacular Thrissur Pooram festival. The elephant procession from this temple is world-famous.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti. Women: Saree or half-saree. Western dress not permitted inside.
temples.photography: Not permitted inside. Festival photography allowed on Pooram day.

temples.name: Haripad Subramanya Temple
temples.description: Famous Subramanya temple in Alappuzha. The annual Ashtami festival draws thousands of devotees. The ten-day Subramanya Ashtami is the most important festival.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu. Women: Saree or churidar. Neat formal dress acceptable.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer precincts only.

temples.name: Payyannur Subramanya Temple
temples.description: Important Subramanya temple in North Kerala famous for the Vela — a brilliant display of fireworks and colourful floats during the annual festival.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat trousers. Women: Saree or churidar. Decent attire required.
temples.photography: Permitted outside temple. Restricted inside sanctum.

temples.name: Madayipara Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Ancient Bhagavathy temple atop a laterite plateau overlooking the Mahe river estuary. Famous for Theyyam performances — the living god tradition of North Kerala.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat clothing. Women: Saree or churidar. Traditional dress preferred during festivals.
temples.photography: Permitted. Theyyam photography allowed with respect for performers.
temples.nearestBus: Payyanur Bus Stand (8 km) · Ezhimala Bus Stop (5 km)

temples.name: Lokanarkavu Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: One of the powerful Bhagavathi temples in the Malabar region. Known for the annual Pooram Mahotsavam and traditional Thoyyam performances.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or trousers. Women: Saree or churidar. Modest dress expected.
temples.photography: Permitted during festival season. Restricted inside shrine.
temples.nearestBus: Moozhikkal Bus Stop (0.3 km) · Kozhikode KSRTC Bus Stand (10 km)

temples.name: Vadakkencherry Mahavishnu Temple
temples.description: Prominent Vishnu temple in the Palakkad region. The Vaikunta Ekadasi festival attracts thousands of devotees from across Kerala.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat formals. Women: Saree or churidar. Traditional dress preferred.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer courtyard. Not permitted near sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Vadakkencherry Bus Stand (0.5 km) · Palakkad KSRTC Bus Stand (20 km)

temples.name: Sasthamkotta Sree Sasta Temple
temples.description: Ancient Sasta temple on the banks of Sasthamkotta Lake — Kerala's largest freshwater lake. A spiritually powerful environment combining nature and devotion.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu. Women: Saree or churidar. Pilgrimage dress for Sabarimala season devotees.
temples.photography: Permitted at the lakeside and outer areas. Restricted inside sanctum.
temples.nearestBus: Sasthamkotta Bus Stand (0.5 km) · Kollam KSRTC Bus Stand (30 km)

temples.name: Kottarakkara Sree Mahaganapathi Temple
temples.description: Renowned Mahaganapathi shrine and cultural centre of Kollam district. Famous for Unniappam offerings and the annual festival with caparisoned elephants.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Kottarakkara Sree Mahaganapathi Temple at Kottarakkara in Kollam district,

temples.name: Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple
temples.description: Forest Sastha temple on the banks of the Kulathupuzha river in the Western Ghats. A serene pilgrimage stop en route to Sabarimala.

temples.name: Ochira Parabrahma Temple
temples.description: Unique open-air temple with no idol or sanctum, worshipping the formless Parabrahma. Famous for Ochira Kali and the annual Ochira festival.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Ammachiveedu Muhurthi Temple
temples.description: Historic family shrine in Anchal associated with local royalty. Known for traditional rituals and the annual Muhurthi festival.

temples.name: Puttingal Devi Temple
temples.description: Major Devi temple in Paravur known for the spectacular fireworks display during the annual festival.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple
temples.description: Rare temple dedicated to Duryodhana on a hilltop (Malanada). No idol — worship is offered to the spirit on the sacred hill.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Aryankavu Ayyappa Temple
temples.description: One of the five sacred Ayyappa temples (Panchasastha) on the Western Ghats. Important stop on the Sabarimala pilgrimage route.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Kottukal Cave Temple
temples.description: Ancient rock-cut cave temple carved from a single massive boulder. A protected monument of early Kerala temple architecture.

temples.name: Umayanalloor Sree Balasubramanya Swamy Temple
temples.description: Prominent Subramanya temple near Kollam town. Known for Skanda Sashti celebrations and traditional Kerala temple architecture.

temples.name: Kadakkal Devi Temple
temples.description: Powerful Devi temple famous for the Kadakkal Thiruvathira and Pongala festivals drawing lakhs of devotees.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Pavumba Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Ancient Mahadeva temple in Karunagappally. A centre of Shiva worship on the Kollam–Alappuzha border.

temples.name: Thirumullavaram Sree Mahavishnu Temple
temples.description: Seaside Vishnu temple near Kollam beach. Known for the annual Arattu festival when the deity is taken to the Arabian Sea.

temples.name: Mayyanad Subramanya Swamy Temple
temples.description: Historic Subramanya temple in Mayyanad with centuries-old traditions and annual Skanda festival.

temples.name: Polachira Devi Temple
temples.description: Bhagavathi temple beside the scenic Polachira wetland. A peaceful shrine popular with local devotees.

temples.name: Thrikkadavoor Sree Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Famous Shiva temple on the banks of Ashtamudi Lake. The Thrikkadavoor Mahashivaratri festival is a major event in Kollam.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Janardanaswamy Temple
temples.description: Ancient cliff-top Vishnu temple overlooking the Arabian Sea at Varkala. Believed to be over 2,000 years old; famous for Arattu at the beach.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Aazhimala Shiva Temple
temples.description: Scenic seaside Shiva temple near Vizhinjam. Known for dramatic coastal views and evening worship.

temples.name: Neyyattinkara Sree Krishna Swamy Temple
temples.description: Important Krishna temple in Neyyattinkara. Centre of Krishna bhakti in southern Thiruvananthapuram district.

temples.name: Sarkara Devi Temple
temples.description: Historic Bhagavathi temple in Chirayinkeezhu. Famous for the grand Sarkara Bharani festival with Kuthiyottam rituals.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.
temples.famousFor: Get the details of the Sarkara Devi Temple, located at Chirayinkeezhu, and the Kaliyoottu festival celebrated

temples.name: Ananthankadu Nagaraja Temple
temples.description: Serpent deity temple in the heart of the city near the Secretariat. Devotees offer milk and turmeric for Nagaraja blessings.

temples.name: Vettucaud Madan Temple
temples.description: Popular Madan (Bhagavathi) temple in Vettucaud. Known for annual festival and community worship traditions.

temples.name: Kadinamkulam Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Mahadeva temple on the shores of Kadinamkulam lake. A serene Shiva shrine in the suburbs of the capital.

temples.name: Karikkakom Chamundi Devi Temple
temples.description: Powerful Chamundi Devi temple in Karikkakom. Famous for Pongala and the annual Chamundi festival.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Pazhakutty Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Traditional Mahadeva temple in Pazhakutty. A local centre of Shiva worship with annual Shivaratri celebrations.

temples.name: Mithranandapuram Venkateswara Temple
temples.description: Venkateswara temple near East Fort. Serves the Vaishnavite community in the historic heart of the city.

temples.name: Karamana Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Ancient Mahadeva temple on the banks of the Karamana river. One of the oldest Shiva shrines in the capital region.

temples.name: Sree Parasurama Temple
temples.description: Sacred temple at the confluence of the Karamana and Parvathy Puthanar rivers. Believed to be the only Parasurama temple in Kerala.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.
temples.famousFor: Get the details of the Sree Parasurama Swami Temple, located at Thiruvallam in Thiruvananthapuram,

temples.name: Madavoorpara Shiva Temple
temples.description: Hilltop Shiva temple at Madavoorpara with rock-cut caves and panoramic views. A growing pilgrimage site near Technopark.

temples.name: Palkulangara Devi Temple
temples.description: Bhagavathi temple in Palkulangara near East Fort. Known for traditional rituals and annual festival.

temples.name: Pandalam Valiya Koyikkal Dharma Sastha Temple
temples.description: Royal temple of the Pandalam dynasty, closely linked to the legend of Lord Ayyappa. The sacred ornaments of Sabarimala are kept here.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Malayalappuzha Devi Temple
temples.description: Powerful Devi temple famous for granting blessings to the childless. The annual festival draws devotees from across Kerala.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Kaviyoor Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: One of the oldest Shiva temples in Kerala with fine mural paintings. A protected monument of archaeological importance.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Thiruvalla Sreevallabha Temple
temples.description: Magnificent Vishnu temple with a circular sanctum and Kathakali as a daily offering. Among the most important Vaishnavite shrines in Kerala.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Kadammanitta Devi Temple
temples.description: Famous for the annual Padayani festival — a spectacular ritual art form performed as an offering to the goddess.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Nilakkal Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Base-camp Shiva temple for Sabarimala pilgrims. Devotees worship here before proceeding to the Ayyappa shrine.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Thumpamon Vadakkumnatha Temple
temples.description: Historic Vadakkumnatha (Shiva) temple in Thumpamon with ancient architecture and annual Shivaratri festival.

temples.name: Kodumthara Subramanya Swamy Temple
temples.description: Subramanya temple in Kodumthara. Known for Skanda Sashti and traditional Kerala temple festivals.

temples.name: Omalloor Rakthakantaswami Temple
temples.description: Vishnu temple in Omalloor known for the annual Vaisheshika festival and centuries-old traditions.

temples.name: Konni Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Mahadeva temple in the forested Konni region. A spiritual centre at the gateway to the Western Ghats.

temples.name: Chengannur Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Ancient temple where the deity is believed to menstruate (Thriputharattu). One of the most unique rituals in Kerala temple tradition.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Mullakkal Rajarajeswari Temple
temples.description: Central Devi temple in Alappuzha town. Famous for the Mullakkal Chirappu festival during Navaratri.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.
temples.famousFor: A page with video glimpses of Mullakkal Chirappu, photo and details of Mullakkal Rajarajeswari temple in Alappuzha district,

temples.name: Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple
temples.description: One of the five ancient Pandava temples in Kerala. A significant Vaishnavite shrine in Chengannur.

temples.name: Thripperunthura Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Historic Mahadeva temple at Thripperumthura near Mannar, listed by TDB as Thripperumthura - Siva under the old Harippad group.
temples.nearestBus: Bus services to Thripperumthura and Mannar

temples.name: Kandiyoor Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Ancient Shiva temple in Mavelikkara, also known as Dakshina Kashi. Features fine mural art and a rich history.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.
temples.famousFor: Get the details of Kandiyoor Mahadeva Temple, located at Mavelikkara in Alappuzha

temples.name: Thuravoor Mahakshetram
temples.description: Twin temple complex with Narasimha and Sudarshana Moorthy. Famous for the annual Narasimha Jayanti celebrations.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Chamakulam Kuttanadu Sree Dharma Sastha Temple
temples.description: Prominent Sastha temple in the Kuttanad backwater region. A spiritual anchor for the farming communities of the area.

temples.name: Karumadi Kuttan Temple
temples.description: Historic site with a black granite statue of Buddha (Karumadi Kuttan) dating to the 9th–14th century. A protected archaeological monument.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Padanilam Parabrahma Temple
temples.description: Open-air Parabrahma temple similar to Ochira. Famous for the spectacular Padanilam Padayani and annual festival.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Evoor Sreekrishnaswamy Temple
temples.description: Krishna temple in Evoor known for the annual Guruvayur Ekadasi celebrations and traditional temple arts.

temples.name: Vettiyar Sree Nagaraja Temple
temples.description: Serpent deity temple in Vettiyar. Devotees offer prayers for fertility and protection from snake-related fears.

temples.name: Sree Krishna Swamy Temple
temples.description: Main Krishna temple in Thodupuzha town. A centre of Vaishnavite worship in central Kerala highlands.

temples.name: Annamala Matha Temple
temples.description: Bhagavathi temple in Vandiperiyar near tea plantations. Popular with plantation workers and travellers to Thekkady.

temples.name: Kanjikuzhi Karikkode Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Bhagavathy temple in Kanjikuzhi. Known for annual festival and traditional percussion ensembles.

temples.name: Ullupooni Sree Krishna Swamy Temple
temples.description: Krishna temple in the high-range village of Ullupooni. A peaceful shrine amid cardamom hills.

temples.name: Mariamman Temple
temples.description: Mariamman temple in Kumily serving the Tamil and Malayali communities near the Periyar wildlife sanctuary.

temples.name: Kallil Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Ancient Jain cave temple later converted to Bhagavathy worship. A protected monument carved from a single rock hill.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer courtyard. Restricted inside sanctum sanctorum.

temples.name: Ayyappancoil Sree Dharma Sastha Temple
temples.description: Sastha temple on the scenic Ayyappancoil hanging bridge over the Periyar river. A picturesque pilgrimage spot.

temples.name: Karikode Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Bhagavathy temple in Karikode near Thodupuzha. Known for Pongala and annual temple festival.

temples.name: Myladumpara Sree Subramanya Temple
temples.description: Subramanya temple in Myladumpara. A hill-country shrine with annual Skanda festival.

temples.name: Kanjiramattom Sri Mahadeva Temple
temples.description: Mahadeva temple near Thodupuzha. Known for Shivaratri celebrations and traditional rituals.

temples.name: Pazhathottam Devi Temple
temples.description: Devi temple in Pazhathottam. A local Bhagavathi shrine in the Idukki foothills.

temples.name: Sree Narasimha Swamy Temple
temples.description: Narasimha temple in Karimannoor. A rare Narasimha shrine in the high ranges of Idukki.

temples.name: Mankulam Bhagavathy Temple
temples.description: Forest-region Bhagavathy temple in Mankulam. A spiritual centre for tribal and settler communities.

temples.name: Kulamavu Devi Temple
temples.description: Devi temple near Kulamavu reservoir. Overlooks the scenic dam and hill country of Idukki.
